symptoms.symptom_name: Cold-triggered painful digital blanching (triphasic colour change)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cold-triggered triphasic colour change is the hallmark of Raynaud's.

symptoms.symptom_name: Colour recovering with warming; no digit fixed white/blue/black

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recovery of colour with warming argues strongly against fixed arterial occlusion.
No fixed white/blue/black digit argues against critical ischaemia, but pulses cannot be checked remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral symmetric cold-triggered attack; no ulcer/gangrene

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetric involvement argues against embolism (usually unilateral).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent GP review for vasodilator therapy (e.g. nifedipine) and CREST management
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Preventive vasodilation reduces attack frequency/severity; commence via GP.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Vascular assessment / nailfold capillaroscopy if recurrent or ischaemic ulcers
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess for digital ischaemia risk in systemic sclerosis.
Only if fixed ischaemia — not indicated for a reversing vasospastic attack.
